title: You Can't Prompt the Room: The Last Skill AI Won't Replace - Balázs Horváth, VisualLabs
summary: Balázs Horváth argues that AI-driven code generation has shifted the bottleneck from implementation to requirements work. Drawing on a VisualLabs hackathon where 17 of 21 agent ideas were abandoned due to poor problem definition rather than technical limits, he proposes that success requires human judgment to read stakeholders, map processes, and frame problems precisely before prompting AI.
writeup: Balázs Horváth of VisualLabs argues that AI code generation has moved the bottleneck from implementation to requirements work, where reading stakeholders, mapping processes, and framing problems precisely now determines outcomes. The claim rests on a VisualLabs hackathon in which 17 of 21 agent ideas were abandoned, with poor problem definition rather than technical limits cited as the cause. The talk covers requirements elicitation, project management, story mapping, a value framework, and agent orchestration, positioning human judgment in defining problems as the skill that survives automation.
